items.title: 'Right fit': Ken Hinkley announced as inaugural Tasmania Devils coach

children.content: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

gallery.altText: Ken Hinkley, wearing a Tasmania Devils AFL jacket, fronts the microphones alongside Grant O'Brien and Brendan Gale.

thumbnail.altText: Ken Hinkley, wearing a Tasmania Devils AFL jacket, fronts the microphones alongside Grant O'Brien and Brendan Gale.

items.title: Hobart Private doctor who does gender-affirming surgery worried over imminent sale to Catholic provider

image.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

children.content: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

items.title: Paramedics told not to take complex cardiac patients to Mersey hospital

children.content: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

image.altText: A hospital exterior

items.title: Despite barely any population growth, Tasmania doesn't have enough houses

children.content: Despite having the slowest growing population in the country, Tasmania is still battling rising homelessness and an ever-tightening rental market.

image.altText: A house model and a key.

items.title: New Delhi police clash with protesters as thousands rally for 'cockroach' movement

children.content: Thousands of supporters of India's youth-led "cockroach" movement gathered in New Delhi to march ‌on parliament seeking the resignation of the education minister, despite police denying permission for the protest.

image.altText: Indian police officers in protective gear push against gathered crowd of protesters holding national flags.

gallery.altText: Crowds of people pushing barricades during a protest at nighttme

thumbnail.altText: Crowds of people pushing barricades during a protest at nighttme

items.title: Plastic waste burning trial at cement factory prompts backlash

children.content: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

image.altText: A person outside the Adbri cement factory.

items.title: Labor MP calls for exclusion zones at synagogues akin to abortion clinics

children.content: Former attorney-general Mark Dreyfus has pushed for more robust social media moderation and protest laws at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

image.altText: Mark Dreyfus in parliament

items.title: 'Good night, sweet dreams': Jacki Weaver leads farewells at Derryn Hinch state funeral

children.content: Broadcaster Ray Martin, Australian actress Jacki Weaver and performer Rhonda Burchmore are among the entertainment heavyweights to pay tribute to Derryn Hinch at his state funeral service.

gallery.altText: The stage and coffin of Derryn Hinch during a State Funeral Service for Derryn Hinch

thumbnail.altText: The stage and coffin of Derryn Hinch during a State Funeral Service for Derryn Hinch

items.title: 'ISIS bride' Rayann El Houli granted bail by Melbourne court

children.content: A woman facing terror charges after living under Islamic State rule in Syria will be freed on bail, after being in custody for two months.

image.altText: Rayann El Houli July court sketch

items.title: Organisers of LGBT club in old church 'devastated' after lease terminated

children.content: Organisers of Divine Playhouse were served a breach notice earlier this month, in the wake of complaints from some members of the Christian community.

image.altText: A gospel choir of drag queens.

items.title: The 13 photos that summed up the chaos of the 2026 World Cup final

children.content: It was magical chaos from start to finish as Spain claimed victory in the 2026 World Cup final. Here are the 13 photos that summed it up.

articles.title: Spain's World Cup win a reminder collective spirit trumps solo stars

children.content: As some of football's biggest names lit up the World Cup, Spain flew under the radar all the way to hoisting the trophy on its final day.

image.altText: A man surrounded by teammates lifts the world cup in celebration

articles.title: Tears streaming down his face, Messi leaves the World Cup stage a legend

children.content: As the champion wept on the pitch after the World Cup final defeat, Argentina may well have wondered with trepidation what life will look like in the post-Messi era.

image.altText: Lionel Messi cries

articles.title: Quick hits: Trump crashes Spain's party, Argentina lose heads in defeat

children.content: Argentina's players lash out in defeat, Ferran Torres becomes a Spanish icon forever and Donald Trump attempts to crash the winners' party. Here are the World Cup final quick hits.

items.title: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors

children.content: A federal law which threatens doctors if they use electronic communications, such as the internet or phone, to discuss voluntary assisted dying remains in force, even though telehealth is now an established part of healthcare.

image.altText: Andrew Denton holds a poster with a VAD law amendment at Parliament House beside VAD advocates Jason and Anthea Smith.

items.title: Decade of work helps remote island's native wildlife 'return to 1616'

children.content: Researchers were unafraid to get their hands "dirty" in an ambitious bid to restore WA's largest island to its pre-colonial state.

image.altText: A small kangaroo-like marsupial jumps on sand.

items.title: Wintry one-pot meals, from cheat's chicken soup to creamy butter beans

children.content: From butter beans with jammy leaks to a quick chicken soup, these recipes are for you if you're on hunt for a low-effort, tasty weeknight meal.

items.title: Why learning to switch off is important for work-life balance

children.content: When being across trends in your industry feels like part of your job it can feel like work never ends. Here's how to protect your free time.

items.title: On the Tiwi Islands, where football is a religion, two teams lift the cup

children.content: The Imalu Tigers and Tapalinga Super Stars are the new premiers of Tiwi Islands football, claiming victory in the men's and women's competitions in electric grand final matches on Sunday.

items.title: I tried to get unapproved injectables from a doctor. It was too easy

children.content: The booming industry selling Australians on treatments to live longer is exploiting significant gaps in health regulation, exposing consumers to risk.

items.title: Why the Tate brothers were arrested and what they are charged with

children.content: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami after UK prosecutors brought further rape and sex trafficking charges against them. Here's what we know about the arrests and extradition request.

image.altText: Andrew Tate, left, watches his brother Tristan speak.

items.title: Abbott amplifies Hanson problem for Liberals

children.content: Pauline Hanson's grievance politics is a challenge for Labor, but a splitting headache for the Liberals — compounded by former prime minister Tony Abbott.

image.altText: A close-up of Tony Abbott speaking at an outdoor event.

items.title: Alone, robbed and in pain: A man finds himself on Sydney's streets

items.title: Lesbian couple told they are no longer the same family by fertility clinic

children.content: Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

items.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader

children.content: South Australia's largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state's most likely to fall to One Nation.

image.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

items.title: 'Like The Castle': Restaurant owners launch petition against land grab

children.content: The owners of a Mount Barker restaurant have launched a petition to block the government's proposed acquisition of the site on which their business stands.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a artistically graffitied wall with his arms crossed.

items.title: Premier says 'witch' billboard dehumanises women, drives violence

children.content: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan says a controversial billboard seen around Melbourne again is corrosive and driving disrespect for women.

items.title: YouTuber Yung Filly violently sexually assaulted young fan, court hears

children.content: A Perth court hears disturbing details of the YouTube personality's alleged sexual assault of a young woman who had a "celebrity crush" on him.

image.altText: Yung Filly shot from the side as he walks into court.

items.title: Former soldier avoids jail for trafficking cocaine in Australian Army

children.content: The former rifleman and field medic sourced cocaine from a fellow Townsville soldier and on-sold the drugs to army colleagues.

image.altText: A young man in a navy suit and medical face mask.

items.title: Breakthrough win for Bernard Tomic after lean tennis times

children.content: Veteran tennis player Bernard Tomic continues to toil on the world circuit and is rewarded with his first Challenger Tour title in eight years.

image.altText: An Australian tennis player tosses the ball in the air and prepares to bring his racquet through to serve.

items.title: Banners at Melbourne game 'disgraceful', Football Victoria says

children.content: A professional football club in Melbourne's north faces calls to ban hardline supporters who brandished sexist and "ethnically derogatory" banners at a match on Saturday.

image.altText: Supporters of the Preston Lions hold up a banner reading "Anti-Hellass".

items.title: For some in Australia, hope is fading and anger is rising
